name: Fred Robbins
abstract: Fred Robbins (born Fred Rubin, September 28, 1919 – June 23, 1992) was an American radio personality who became a television host and celebrity interviewer.

concerts.label: Fred Robbins' One-Night Stand: Louis Armstrong
Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Poll Winners Jazz Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Wilber
about_quotes.evidence: the promoter Ernie Anderson and Fred Robbins who was a big jazz disc jockey at the time, they were running around saying, “Where’s Bechet! Where’s Bechet! Bob, you live with him, where is he?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Wilber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Varro
about_quotes.evidence: later on Fred Robbins took over and was playing mostly jazz... Fred Robbins might have been one of the first to put a lot of jazz together
about_quotes.doc_title: Johnny Varro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Armstrong
about_quotes.evidence: Fred Robbins, a jazz critic, was the last person outside the family who saw the King of Jazz last Sunday on his 71st birthday.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank "Machito" Grillo
about_quotes.evidence: Well, yes, I used to listen to Fred Robbins because he always had a good musical program.

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: I don't know who named it after Fred Robbins, the disk jockey there...
